(33) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Extras: Updating the NexT Theme
This post covers updating the NexT theme and what's changed in NexT after Hexo 5.
In the previous post I mentioned that Hexo 5 themes can be installed via npm, and NexT is now available on npm too. The main benefit here is
and merging — not exactly smooth. The theme had to be version-controlled, and making small layout tweaks was awkward.
So now we'll install NexT via npm.

approach if you plan to customise heavily — but you'd want the files under
Quick concept first: npm-installed files live under
. What does this mean? Earlier in "
Learning Hexo from Scratch - Understanding the Hexo Directory Structure

" I mentioned this folder is a "cache for the packages Hexo needs to run". We don't touch or modify anything in there because it's restored via npm. The upside is convenient updates and easier configuration — you'll feel it more once we get into the next bit.
The old approach is, as I said, a pain to update. Version control was tricky — themes under
couldn't be tracked properly without deleting the nested
folder first, otherwise you'd hit issues when switching machines.
What's new in the latest NexT
NexT's config story changed after Hexo 5. You won't find a
— when installing via npm, you have to create your own theme config. Per the official docs, run this from your Hexo project root:
in the Hexo root — that's now your theme config file.

The contents are mostly the same. The most distinctive new feature is around customisation. Before, customising meant directly editing NexT's source files, which broke your ability to update the theme. The latest NexT supports customisation via designated files, including these (excerpt from

custom_file_path:
  #head: source/_data/head.njk # head
  #header: source/_data/header.njk
  #sidebar: source/_data/sidebar.njk # sidebar
  #postMeta: source/_data/post-meta.njk # below the article title
  #postBodyEnd: source/_data/post-body-end.njk # end of article
  #footer: source/_data/footer.njk # footer
  #bodyEnd: source/_data/body-end.njk
  #variable: source/_data/variables.styl
  #mixin: source/_data/mixins.styl
  #style: source/_data/styles.styl
(Also worth noting — this version of NexT switched templates to njk.)
So as you can guess — your customisations now live under
instead of editing NexT's source code directly. Big difference.
Adding stuff is straightforward. For example, to put a Google Ad snippet at the end of every article, I'd just add this to
For the Google AdSense config, add this at the end of

# Google AdSense
google_adsense:
  enable: true
  google_ad_client_id: #
  enable_page_level_ads: true
  ## sidebar
  siderbar_enable: true
  siderbar_data_ad_slot: #
  ## post header
  postheader_enable: true
  postheader_data_ad_slot: #
  ## post footer
  postfooter_enable: true
  postfooter_data_ad_slot: #
The concept is the same as my earlier post "
Adding Google AdSense to Hexo NexT (article in Mandarin)
" — this section still needs some tweaking.

Back to the main point: this approach lets you customise the theme heavily without touching NexT's source code, and config management is much easier. I'll skip the rest of the details — the official docs cover them well:
